changes.check: Stage 2A — Estate inventory file actual location
changes.detail: The prompt (Phase 1, 'Known likely files to inspect' section, line 187) lists 'knowledge_bus/package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml' as a likely file to inspect. The actual file is at 'knowledge_bus/governance/package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml' (confirmed present by directory search, and authoritative in kb_lifecycle_contract_v1.py line 56). The STOP condition (Phase 3) and runtime orphan detection will function correctly as the code uses the correct path.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Intelligence Core components in scope
changes.detail: Intelligence Core components directly affected: (1) backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py — _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS list (line 76) and compile_root_cause_v1 entrypoint (line 485) are the primary behavioural surface. (2) backend/core/knowledge/load_root_cause_hypotheses.py — hypothesis loaders, potentially read-only but Cursor may add new loader infrastructure if metadata-driven registration proceeds. (3) backend/core/knowledge/kb_lifecycle_contract_v1.py — potentially updated to add WHY-registration metadata validation. (4) backend/scripts/validate_kb_package_estate_orphans_v1.py — may receive enhancements. Risk: any change to _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS list directly affects which signals produce governed WHY output.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Behavioural surface area and expected output changes
changes.detail: Phases 1–4 are documentation/fingerprint/assessment only — zero runtime change expected. Implementation (Phase 5) may change registration mechanism. Expected output: NONE unless Phase 5 proceeds. Core rule (prompt §Core rule): 'Every currently registered root-cause / WHY target that works before this sprint must still load and produce equivalent WHY output after this sprint.' Fallback mechanism (_compile_why_engine_fallback_finding) must remain intact for signals not in the registered list. Before/after fingerprint (Phases 2 and 5) is the contract — any delta is a STOP.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Regression targets: 41 registered targets + fallback + homocysteine
changes.citation: All 41 targets confirmed in _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS (root_cause_compiler_v1.py lines 77–118). signal_homocysteine_high and signal_homocysteine_elevation_context both registered (lines 77–78) — homocysteine lead finding protected. Fallback mechanism at line 438 protects unregistered signals. All prior scaffold/launch-core guards backed by confirmed test files.

changes.detail: Phase 4 mandatory review checkpoint is correctly specified in the prompt. Cursor must STOP after writing the proposed mechanism and report 'LC-S18 proposed WHY registration mechanism ready for architectural review' before implementing any registration behaviour change. Cursor may not self-authorise. This gate is correctly positioned.

changes.check: Stage 2B — STOP condition: Phase 3 estate drift
changes.detail: Phase 3 STOP condition is correctly specified: if auto-discovery would load orphan packages with WHY assets, or if lifecycle state cannot distinguish validated from draft assets, STOP and report whether LC-S18A (estate inventory refresh) is needed. This is a deterministic gate — the orphan reporter script (validate_kb_package_estate_orphans_v1.py) provides the data. Note: reporter exits non-zero if any orphans exist; prior known drift from LC-S16/17/19 (109 packages) must be assessed before triggering STOP.

Forbidden files explicitly prohibit: backend/core/scoring/**, backend/core/units/**, backend/ssot/units.yaml, backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml, backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml, all frontend/**, all automation_bus/state/*, automation_bus gate files, kernel/gate scripts. Knowledge Bus files (knowledge_bus/package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml, knowledge_bus/packages/**) allowed only if metadata validation absolutely requires non-medical additions AND GPT explicitly approves after Phase 4 review checkpoint. No path from authorised work to forbidden files detectable.

Primary risk: if any change to _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS list or loader pattern silently drops a target, the before/after fingerprint comparison will catch it. Phase 2 (before-fingerprint) and post-Phase 5 (after-fingerprint) are mandatory and will surface any drift. lru_cache on hypothesis loaders means ordering must remain deterministic. Duplicate target IDs must fail loudly — the prompt requires this explicitly. No silent skip behaviour is allowed.

changes.check: Stage 2B — No-op check: real work exists
changes.detail: Not a no-op sprint. Real work exists: (1) _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS is a 41-entry manually maintained list with no validation layer; (2) no before/after fingerprint infrastructure exists; (3) no LC-S18 notes document exists; (4) 7 new Sentinel defect classes are required and absent; (5) no test_lc_s18_root_cause_why_registration.py exists; (6) no WHY-enabled package metadata validation exists. Sprint is fully justified.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. risk_level HIGH and change_type BEHAVIOUR correctly classified for Intelligence Core root-cause compiler work. All mandatory and optional authority paths confirmed present. Root-cause compiler read: 41 registered targets in _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS (root_cause_compiler_v1.py lines 76–118), single registration authority, fallback mechanism present. All 8 cross-sprint regression test files confirmed present. Sentinel pack has 17 existing defect classes; 7 new classes required, none currently present. One warning: prompt body (Phase 1 'Known likely files') lists estate inventory at 'knowledge_bus/package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml' which does not exist — actual authoritative path is 'knowledge_bus/governance/package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml' per kb_lifecycle_contract_v1.py line 56. Runtime code is correct; Cursor must use the authoritative path. No blockers. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start.
